_comment: Corpus doré minuscule et FIXE pour le garde-fou anti-régression. Entité-riche (le graphe s'engage), requêtes sans ambiguïté (récupération robuste/déterministe). Ne PAS modifier sans régénérer eval/baselines.json (python -m eval.check_regression --update).

docs.text: Marie Curie was a physicist and chemist who conducted pioneering research on radioactivity. She remains the only person to win Nobel Prizes in two different sciences, Physics and Chemistry.
The Eiffel Tower is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. It was designed by the engineer Gustave Eiffel for the 1889 World's Fair.
Mount Everest, in the Himalayas on the border between Nepal and China, is Earth's highest mountain above sea level, at 8,849 metres.
Python is a high-level programming language created by Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991. It emphasizes code readability and a simple syntax.
The Amazon River in South America is the largest river in the world by discharge volume of water, carrying more water than the next several rivers combined.
William Shakespeare was an English playwright and poet, widely regarded as the greatest writer in the English language. He wrote Hamlet, Macbeth and Romeo and Juliet.
The Great Wall of China is a series of fortifications built across the historical northern borders of ancient Chinese states to protect against nomadic invasions.
Albert Einstein developed the theory of relativity, a pillar of modern physics. His equation E=mc squared expresses the equivalence of mass and energy.
The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of Earth's oceans, extending from the Arctic in the north to the Southern Ocean, separating Asia and the Americas.
Leonardo da Vinci was an Italian polymath of the Renaissance. He painted the Mona Lisa and The Last Supper, and filled notebooks with scientific drawings.
The Sahara is the largest hot desert in the world, covering much of North Africa, with an area comparable to that of the United States or China.
The Tokyo metropolitan area in Japan is the most populous metropolitan area in the world, serving as the country's political and economic centre.

queries.text: Which scientist won Nobel Prizes in both Physics and Chemistry for radioactivity research?
Who designed the iron tower built in Paris for the 1889 World's Fair?
What is the highest mountain on Earth and where is it located?
Who created the Python programming language?
Which river has the largest water discharge in the world?
Who wrote the plays Hamlet and Romeo and Juliet?
Which scientist developed the theory of relativity and the equation E=mc squared?
Who painted the Mona Lisa during the Renaissance?
